title: Superconducting and correlated phases of an effective Hubbard model on the BCC lattice
abstract: We investigate the electronic phases of an effective Hubbard model on the body-centered-cubic lattice, motivated by alkali-doped fulleride molecular solids. The model incorporates renormalized on-site interactions and an effective inverted Hund's coupling originating from electron-phonon interactions. To access complementary interaction regimes, we employ two theoretical approaches. In the intermediate-coupling regime, the on-site repulsive interaction is approximated by a long-range interaction in momentum space, yielding an exactly solvable Hatsugai-Kohmoto model supplemented by a BCS-type pairing term. Within this framework, we analyze the superconducting instability and demonstrate a first-order normal-superconducting phase transition, characterized by a discontinuous jump of the order parameter. In the strong-coupling regime, where pairing fluctuations are suppressed, we apply the spin rotationally invariant slave-boson formalism to map out the temperature-interaction phase diagram. This analysis reveals first-order transitions between a Fermi-liquid phase, an antiferromagnetic phase, and a Mott insulating phase, with a narrow intermediate region where all three phases compete. The resulting phase diagram captures the interplay of itinerancy, magnetic order, and Mott localization in three dimensions and provides a unified perspective on superconducting and correlation-driven phenomena in fulleride-inspired lattice systems.
summary: This paper explores the complex phase diagram of a 3D Hubbard model on a BCC lattice, which serves as a model for alkali-doped fullerides. It reveals that the transition to superconductivity is first-order and identifies a multicritical region where Mott insulating, antiferromagnetic, and Fermi-liquid phases coexist. The work provides a unified perspective on how lattice geometry and electron-phonon-mediated interactions drive competing electronic orders.
main_problem: Investigating the competition and interplay between superconducting, magnetic, and Mott insulating phases in a 3D BCC lattice, specifically motivated by alkali-doped fulleride molecular solids.
main_result: The study demonstrates a first-order normal-to-superconducting phase transition and identifies a temperature-interaction phase diagram in the strong-coupling regime featuring a narrow region where Fermi-liquid, antiferromagnetic, and Mott insulating phases compete.
method: The authors employ two approaches: the Hatsugai-Kohmoto model supplemented by a BCS-type pairing term for the intermediate-coupling regime, and a spin-rotationally invariant slave-boson formalism for the strong-coupling regime.
model_or_system: An effective Hubbard model on a body-centered-cubic (BCC) lattice, incorporating renormalized on-site Coulomb interactions (U_eff) and an effective inverted Hund's coupling (J_eff) originating from electron-phonon interactions.
key_observables: Superconducting order parameter (delta), critical temperature (T_C), quasiparticle weight, and free energy.
important_parameters: Effective on-site interaction (U_eff), effective Hund's coupling (J_eff), hopping amplitude (t), and the super-exchange interaction scale (J_ex).
main_assumptions: Neglects off-site Coulomb interactions, spin-flip interactions, and orbital degrees of freedom; assumes diagonal electron hopping between molecular orbitals; uses the anti-adiabatic limit for electron-phonon interactions.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows effective on-site interactions as a function of C60 volume; Figure 6 shows a three-phase structure for the BCC lattice similar to a 2D square lattice.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the physical motivation (fullerides), defines the effective Hubbard model, presents the intermediate-coupling analysis using the HK-BCS model, details the strong-coupling analysis using the slave-boson approach, and concludes with a discussion of the resulting phase diagram and limitations.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1: Effective intra-orbital (black circles) and inter-orbital (gray squares) on-site interactions. These Coulomb interactions are renormalized due electron-phonon interactions. For these calculation, the bare interaction parameters are taken from ab initio calculations from ref. [40] and presented in ref. [7].
FIG. 2: Density of states for the nearest-neighbor only BCC lattice

FIG. 3: Helmholtz free energy difference as a function of superconducting order parameter ∆at Ueff = t and Jeff = −2t. As temperature (kBT) decreases from 0.2t to 0.1t (Top to bottom: 0.2t, 0.18t, 0.15t, 0.1t), the secondary local minimum in the free energy becomes the global minimum indicating a first order phase transition from normal phase to superconducting phase.

FIG. 5: Critical temperature for superconductor-Normal phase transition for Jeff = −2t. The normal-superconductor phase transition is of second order for 0 < Ueff < 0.3t (upto maximum) second order and it becomes first order for larger values of Ueff.

FIG. 4: Superconducting order parameter ∆/t as a function of temperature kBT/t at Ueff = t and Jeff = −2t. The jump in the order parameter at kBT ≃0.18t indicates the first order superconductor-Normal phase transition.
